title: achillea sylvestris
intro: Achillea sylvestris, also known as woolly milfoil, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the aster family, native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing pain, inflammation, and improving wound healing. The leaves and flowers of the plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties. The plant's health benefits also extend to its use in treating digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine.
